reasoning: 8x zoom reveals two primary tells. (1) Inconsistent outline width: the dark border separating the orange ring from the black background alternates between 1px and 2px with no clear artistic logic — top-left edge is 1px, bottom-right transitions show a 2px dark fringe. Human pixel artists plan border weight deliberately; the irregularity pattern matches AI weight randomness. (2) Asymmetric particle scatter: the spark/debris pixels ringing the explosion are notably denser in the bottom-right quadrant with gaps top-left that aren't mirrored. A human VFX artist making a static frame would either use rotational symmetry or a clearly intentional asymmetric composition; this distribution looks stochastic. On the positive side: no sub-pixel or AA artifacts (clean integer pixel grid), no noise in solid dark background, palette is disciplined (3-4 hard tones per zone with no gradient bleed), the checkerboard shockwave interior is a legitimate pixel-art technique, and the central star silhouette reads semantically correct. The two structural tells are real but not catastrophic — score sits at the marginal/fail boundary.
